summary.intent: Fix issue #430 in the moltis repo where some LLMs (e.g., Qwen3-Coder) send cron tool parameters as JSON strings instead of objects, or splay nested fields at the top level, causing 'job must be an object' and 'missing job parameter' errors.
summary.outcome: Implemented three rescue paths in cron_tool.rs: a try_parse_stringified_object helper, inline string-to-object coercion in normalize_*_value functions, and flat parameter rescue in execute(). Added 5 new tests; all 27 tests pass. Code was committed, pushed, and a PR was created.

repo: The moltis cron tool is in crates/tools/src/cron_tool.rs and has normalize_job_value, normalize_patch_value, normalize_schedule_value, normalize_payload_value, and normalize_sandbox_value functions for input coercion.
The worktree path for this feature branch is /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/uncovered-kip/
LLM tool-call robustness is an explicit concern: the cron tool must handle malformed but recoverable inputs from weaker models.

code.finding: normalize_job_value() — added try_parse_stringified_object call before .as_object_mut() check to handle stringified JSON objects sent by LLMs
normalize_patch_value() — same stringified object rescue applied here
execute() 'add' arm — flat parameter rescue: if params.get('job') is None but top-level params contain schedule/payload keys, assemble synthetic job object

workflow: Rust &mut parameter pattern matching in normalize functions required careful handling — initial implementation had a compile error that needed fixing before tests passed.
Running cargo fmt after edits is necessary; the project enforces formatting.

friction: Initial implementation of the normalize function coercions had a &mut pattern matching error that required three separate edit passes to fix.
Required multiple reads of the same file (6 reads of cron_tool.rs during analysis) due to the file being large and needing different sections.
open_items: The same LLM parameter coercion problem may exist in other tools beyond cron_tool — no audit was done of other tool implementations.
The flat parameter rescue only covers the 'add' arm; the 'update' arm may have the same issue but wasn't addressed in this fix.
No changes were made to the tool schema to better guide LLMs — documentation/description improvements could reduce these errors at the source.
